mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering recession risk premiums that suppress equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy > 55% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield a positive 60-day return in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a confirmed economic expansion reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating short-term positive momentum in equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes yield negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of expansion-phase observations, or rolling Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation readings above 2.5 percent trigger expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger instances, or the negative correlation between CPIAUCSL levels and equity returns breaks down.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll employment growth reflects a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, creating a persistent upward drift in equity valuations over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03, or positive payroll changes coincide with negative equity returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales expansion validate strong aggregate demand, reducing default risk and boosting earnings forecasts, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined positive signal from GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative 20-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent indicates a sudden supply-side contraction or demand shock, prompting risk-off positioning and generating negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered decline instances, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.
